summary: After a year-long blogging hiatus, Yegge returns to reflect on what he learned: caring too much about reader reactions was damaging his health, haters are inevitable (Reddit proves nothing gets above 80% approval), and his posts were indeed too long. The bulk of the post details his experience with Trigger Finger, a repetitive strain injury he acquired from obsessively practicing a Villa-Lobos guitar etude approximately 650,000 times. After cortisone shots and eventual surgery, his hand recovered to about 95%, teaching him the value of moderation in all pursuits. He plans to return to tech blogging with a lighter touch and shorter posts.
key_points: Haters are a statistical inevitability - even successful Reddit posts only reach 65-80% approval, so there's no point wasting energy on them
Trigger Finger is the second most common hand ailment after Carpal Tunnel, often caused by repetitive overuse in musicians and typists
The obsessive personality that drives someone to practice an arpeggio 650,000 times in 5 months is the same one that leads to overly long blog posts and caring too much about reader reactions
Physical therapy and surgery can't fully erase the fear - his hand still twists to avoid pain that's mostly gone, a metaphor for psychological damage
Forced moderation from injury led to positive changes: sustainable guitar style, better typing economy, and Emacs shortcuts he wishes he'd learned sooner
money_quotes: There is nothing on this earth that can make everyone happy.
Caring is fine. Lots of things are worth caring about. Very few of them merit sacrificing your health.

If they ever make a movie about my life, the handsome and dashing actor who plays me, when asked on his deathbed which of life's pleasures had given him the greatest happiness, will say something cheesy that makes the audience ooh and aww with appreciation, but it'll be total Hollywood bullshit, because what I really will have said was 'gaming'.

Its more formal name is digital tenovaginitis stenosans, which is ancient Latin for 'electronic hand inflamed vagina without writing', which I believe is why most people prefer to call it Trigger Finger.
Trigger Finger is 'idiopathic', a fancy word meaning that doctors don't have a fucking clue what causes it.
Your body will begin telling you when it's time to ease up on the practice sessions. Listen to your body when it says that.
In the last fifteen minutes my whole life had been handed back to me with an almost casual lack of concern.
My right-hand fingers twist and curl in elaborate, incomprehensible dances to avoid a pain that is for the most part no longer there.
key_insight: The same obsessive drive that makes someone successful can also destroy them if they don't learn moderation - whether it's practicing guitar 650,000 times or caring too much about blog post reactions.
